title: Authors and Citation
text: Guillaume Noblet. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Noblet G (2025). AddFonts: Add Fonts Several Providers 'showtext'. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/gnoblet/AddFonts.
code: @Manual{,   title = {AddFonts: Add Fonts from Several Providers to 'showtext'},   author = {Guillaume Noblet},   year = {2025},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/gnoblet/AddFonts}, }

what: Add Fonts from Several Providers to showtext
title: Add Fonts from Several Providers to showtext
text: AddFonts provides easy way download use fonts various providers R graphics. Currently supports Bunny Fonts, privacy-focused, GDPR-compliant alternative Google Fonts.

title: Add Fonts from Several Providers to showtext
text: 🔒 Privacy-focused: Use Bunny Fonts instead Google Fonts 📦 Simple: Similar showtext::font_add_google() 💾 Smart caching: Downloads fonts , uses everywhere 🔍 Easy discovery: Search fonts name category 🎨 Full support: Regular, bold, italic, bold-italic variants

title: Add Fonts from Several Providers to showtext
text: can install development version AddFonts GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("gnoblet/AddFonts")

title: Add Fonts from Several Providers to showtext
text: AddFonts requires woff2_decompress command-line tool convert downloaded font files format compatible R graphics. Install using system’s package manager:
code: # macOS (Homebrew) brew install woff2  # Debian/Ubuntu sudo apt install woff2  # Fedora/RHEL sudo dnf install woff2-tools  # Arch Linux sudo pacman -S woff2  # Windows # Build from source: https://github.com/google/woff2

title: Add Fonts from Several Providers to showtext
code: library(AddFonts) #> AddFonts loaded successfully. woff2_decompress found at: /usr/bin/woff2_decompress library(showtext) #> Loading required package: sysfonts #> Loading required package: showtextdb  # Add a font from Bunny Fonts (downloads all weights and styles by default) add_font("open-sans")  # Or use the provider-specific function add_font_bunny("roboto")  # Enable showtext for graphics showtext_auto()  # Use the font in your plots plot(1:10, main = "Using Open Sans from Bunny Fonts!")

title: Add Fonts from Several Providers to showtext
code: library(ggplot2) library(AddFonts) library(showtext)  # Add custom fonts with specific weights add_font_bunny(   "Advent Pro",   family = "main_font",   regular_wt = 400,   bold_wt = 700 )  showtext_auto()  ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   labs(     title = "Motor Trend Car Road Tests",     subtitle = "Relationship between weight and fuel efficiency"   ) +   theme_minimal(base_family = "main_font") +   theme(     plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),     plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 14)   )

title: Add Fonts from Several Providers to showtext
text: Find fonts name browse category:
code: # Search for fonts by name (uses default provider: Bunny) font_search("roboto") #>            familyName   category                                     weights #> 1379           Roboto sans-serif                100, 300, 400, 500, 700, 900 #> 1380 Roboto Condensed sans-serif 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900 #> 1381      Roboto Flex sans-serif                                         400 #> 1382      Roboto Mono  monospace           100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700 #> 1383     Roboto Serif      serif 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900 #> 1384      Roboto Slab      serif 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900  # Or use provider-specific function font_search_bunny("roboto") #>            familyName   category                                     weights #> 1379           Roboto sans-serif                100, 300, 400, 500, 700, 900 #> 1380 Roboto Condensed sans-serif 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900 #> 1381      Roboto Flex sans-serif 1384      Roboto Slab      serif 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900  # List all available fonts fonts <- font_list() head(fonts) #>           family     familyName   category variants.latin title: Add Fonts from Several Providers to showtext
code: # Download all available weights and styles (default) add_font("roboto")  # Download only specific weights add_font("roboto", wt = c(300, 400, 700))  # Download only normal (non-italic) styles add_font("roboto", wt = c(400, 700), styles = "normal")  # Download only italic styles with specific weights add_font("merriweather", wt = c(400, 700), styles = "italic")  # Download a single weight add_font("source-code-pro", wt = 400, styles = "normal")  # Specify a different character subset add_font("noto-sans", subset = "latin-ext")  # You can also use provider-specific functions add_font_bunny("inter", wt = c(300, 500), styles = "normal")

title: Add Fonts from Several Providers to showtext
text: default, fonts cached platform-appropriate directory. can customize :
code: # Check current cache directory get_font_cache_dir() #> [1] "~/.cache/AddFonts"  # Use a custom cache location add_font_bunny("open-sans", cache_dir = "~/my-fonts")  # Clear the font cache clear_font_cache_dir() #> ℹ No cached fonts found in '~/.cache/AddFonts' #> [1] TRUE

title: Add Fonts from Several Providers to showtext
text: AddFonts includes bundled database 1,700+ fonts Bunny Fonts, updated monthly via GitHub Actions. means: ✅ API calls needed browsing ✅ Fast font search listing ✅ Works offline (initial font download) ✅ Automatically stays --date database regenerated monthly using Python script:
code: # Update the font database manually uv run scripts/create_bunny_font_db.py

title: Add Fonts from Several Providers to showtext
text: Bunny Fonts open-source, privacy-first alternative Google Fonts: 🔒 GDPR compliant: tracking, data collection 🚀 Fast CDN: Globally distributed network 🆓 Free & open: accounts API keys needed 🎨 Full coverage: Includes popular Google Fonts Perfect users EU anyone concerned privacy.

title: Add Fonts from Several Providers to showtext
text: Search: Browse bundled font database (updated monthly) Download: Fetch WOFF2 font files Bunny’s CDN (cached locally) Convert: Automatically convert WOFF2 TTF format R compatibility Register: Add fonts R via sysfonts Use: Enable showtext_auto() use graphics device conversion? Bunny Fonts provides fonts WOFF2 format (optimized web use), R’s sysfonts doesn’t support WOFF2 ---box. AddFonts automatically converts downloaded fonts TTF format maximum compatibility. Performance: Conversion happens per font file. Subsequent uses font load instantly TTF cache.

title: Add Fonts from Several Providers to showtext
text: showtext: Using fonts R graphics sysfonts: Loading fonts R systemfonts: System font management extrafont: TrueType font support

title: Add Fonts from Several Providers to showtext
text: Note: AddFonts early development. Everything may change. Feedback contributions welcome!

what: Add Fonts from Multiple Providers — add_font
title: Add Fonts from Multiple Providers — add_font
text: general function download register fonts various providers. Currently supports Bunny Fonts, providers planned future.

title: Add Fonts from Multiple Providers — add_font
code: add_font(   name,   provider = "bunny",   family = NULL,   wt = NULL,   styles = "both",   ... )

title: Add Fonts from Multiple Providers — add_font
text: name (character(1)) font name listed provider. provider (character(1)) Font provider use. Currently supports "bunny" Bunny Fonts, privacy-focused Google Fonts alternative (default: "bunny"). family (character(1) NULL) family name register R (default: NULL, uses name). wt (numeric NULL) Font weights download (e.g., c(400, 700)). Use vector specify certain weights (e.g., c(300, 500, 700)) (default: NULL, downloads available weights). styles (character(1)) styles download: "normal", "italic", "". Set "normal" regular styles , "italic" italic , "" available styles (default: ""). ... Additional provider-specific arguments.

title: Add Fonts from Multiple Providers — add_font
text: (: list) Invisibly returns list paths registered font files.

title: Add Fonts from Multiple Providers — add_font
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Add a font from Bunny Fonts (default provider) with all weights/styles add_font("roboto")  # Add only specific weights add_font("open-sans", wt = c(400, 700))  # Add only normal (non-italic) styles add_font("inter", wt = c(300, 400, 700), styles = "normal")  # Add italic styles only with custom family name add_font("merriweather", family = "Merri", wt = c(400, 700), styles = "italic")  # Explicitly specify provider add_font("source-code-pro", provider = "bunny", wt = 400)  # Enable showtext and use the font showtext::showtext_auto() plot(1:10, main = "Using fonts from multiple providers!") } # }

what: Download and Register a Font from Bunny Fonts — add_font_bunny
title: Download and Register a Font from Bunny Fonts — add_font_bunny
text: Downloads font fonts.bunny.net, caches locally, registers use R graphics devices via sysfonts showtext. provides privacy-focused alternative Google Fonts.

title: Download and Register a Font from Bunny Fonts — add_font_bunny
code: add_font_bunny(   name,   family = NULL,   wt = NULL,   styles = "both",   regular.wt = NULL,   bold.wt = NULL,   subset = NULL,   cache_dir = NULL,   ... )

title: Download and Register a Font from Bunny Fonts — add_font_bunny
text: name (character(1)) font name listed Bunny Fonts (e.g., "open-sans"). Can family ID (lowercase hyphens) display name (case-insensitive). family (character(1) NULL) family name register R (default: NULL, uses name). wt (numeric NULL) Font weights download (e.g., c(400, 700)). Use vector specify certain weights (e.g., c(300, 500, 700)) (default: NULL, downloads available weights). styles (character(1)) styles download: "normal", "italic", "". Set "normal" regular styles , "italic" italic , "" available styles (default: ""). regular.wt (numeric(1) NULL) weight use regular font face sysfonts::font_add() (default: NULL, uses first weight wt). bold.wt (numeric(1) NULL) weight use bold font face sysfonts::font_add() (default: NULL, uses last weight wt). subset (character(1) NULL) Font subset use (e.g., "latin", "latin-ext") (default: NULL, uses font's default subset). cache_dir (character(1) NULL) Directory cache downloaded fonts (default: NULL, uses platform-appropriate cache directory via get_font_cache_dir()). ... Additional arguments passed sysfonts::font_add().

title: Download and Register a Font from Bunny Fonts — add_font_bunny
text: (: list) Invisibly returns list paths registered font files.

title: Download and Register a Font from Bunny Fonts — add_font_bunny
text: Font files downloaded WOFF2 automatically converted TTF format compatibility sysfonts. requires woff2_decompress command-line tool installed system.

title: Download and Register a Font from Bunny Fonts — add_font_bunny
text: function requires woff2_decompress command-line tool convert downloaded WOFF2 files TTF format. Install using: macOS: brew install woff2 Debian/Ubuntu: sudo apt install woff2 Fedora/RHEL: sudo dnf install woff2-tools Arch Linux: sudo pacman -S woff2 Windows: Build https://github.com/google/woff2

title: Download and Register a Font from Bunny Fonts — add_font_bunny
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Add a font with all weights and styles (default) add_font_bunny("open-sans")  # Add only specific weights add_font_bunny("roboto", wt = c(300, 400, 700))  # Add only normal (non-italic) styles add_font_bunny("roboto", styles = "normal")  # Add specific weights in italic only add_font_bunny("merriweather", wt = c(400, 700), styles = "italic")  # Specify which weights to use for regular and bold add_font_bunny("roboto", wt = c(300, 400, 700), regular.wt = 400, bold.wt = 700)  # Specify custom family name add_font_bunny("source-code-pro", family = "SourceCode", wt = 400)  # Enable showtext and use the font showtext::showtext_auto() plot(1:10, main = "This uses fonts from Bunny Fonts!") } # }

title: Clear Font Cache — clear_font_cache_dir
text: Removes cached Bunny Fonts free space force re-download.

title: Clear Font Cache — clear_font_cache_dir
code: clear_font_cache_dir(confirm = TRUE)

title: Clear Font Cache — clear_font_cache_dir
text: confirm (logical(1)) Whether ask confirmation deleting cached files (default: TRUE).

title: Clear Font Cache — clear_font_cache_dir
text: (: logical(1)) TRUE cache cleared successfully, FALSE otherwise.

title: Clear Font Cache — clear_font_cache_dir
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Clear cache with confirmation clear_font_cache_dir()  # Clear cache without confirmation clear_font_cache_dir(confirm = FALSE) } # }

what: List Fonts from Multiple Providers — font_list
title: List Fonts from Multiple Providers — font_list
text: general function list available fonts various providers. Currently supports Bunny Fonts, providers planned future.

title: List Fonts from Multiple Providers — font_list
code: font_list(provider = "bunny", ...)

title: List Fonts from Multiple Providers — font_list
text: provider (character(1)) Font provider query. Currently supports "bunny" Bunny Fonts (default: "bunny"). ... Additional provider-specific arguments (currently unused).

title: List Fonts from Multiple Providers — font_list
text: (: data.frame) data.frame font metadata specified provider.

title: List Fonts from Multiple Providers — font_list
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # List all fonts from default provider (Bunny) fonts <- font_list() head(fonts)  # Explicitly specify provider bunny_fonts <- font_list(provider = "bunny")  # Count fonts by category table(fonts$category) } # }

what: List Available Fonts from Bunny.net (Direct Function) — font_list_bunny
title: List Available Fonts from Bunny.net (Direct Function) — font_list_bunny
text: Retrieve list fonts available fonts.bunny.net using package's bundled database. convenience function directly returns Bunny Fonts without going generic interface.

title: List Available Fonts from Bunny.net (Direct Function) — font_list_bunny
code: font_list_bunny()

title: List Available Fonts from Bunny.net (Direct Function) — font_list_bunny
text: data.frame columns: family, familyName, category, weights, styles, defSubset, isVariable, url representing fonts metadata Bunny Fonts.

what: Search Fonts from Multiple Providers — font_search
title: Search Fonts from Multiple Providers — font_search
text: general function search available fonts various providers. Currently supports Bunny Fonts, providers planned future.

title: Search Fonts from Multiple Providers — font_search
code: font_search(query = NULL, provider = "bunny", category = NULL, ...)

title: Search Fonts from Multiple Providers — font_search
text: query (character(1) NULL) String search font names categories (default: NULL, returns fonts, optionally filtered category). provider (character(1)) Font provider search. Currently supports "bunny" Bunny Fonts (default: "bunny"). category (character(1) NULL) Filter category (provider-specific). Bunny Fonts: "sans-serif", "serif", "display", "handwriting", "monospace" (default: NULL, categories). ... Additional provider-specific arguments (currently unused).

title: Search Fonts from Multiple Providers — font_search
text: (: data.frame) data.frame matching font metadata.

title: Search Fonts from Multiple Providers — font_search
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Search all providers (currently just Bunny) font_search("roboto")  # Search within a specific category font_search("sans", category = "sans-serif")  # List all monospace fonts font_search(category = "monospace")  # Explicitly specify provider font_search("inter", provider = "bunny") } # }

what: Search Bunny Fonts by Name or Category (Direct Function) — font_search_bunny
title: Search Bunny Fonts by Name or Category (Direct Function) — font_search_bunny
text: Search fonts available Bunny Fonts name, category, attributes. convenience function directly searches Bunny Fonts without going generic interface.

title: Search Bunny Fonts by Name or Category (Direct Function) — font_search_bunny
code: font_search_bunny(query = NULL, category = NULL)

title: Search Bunny Fonts by Name or Category (Direct Function) — font_search_bunny
text: query Character string search font names categories. NULL empty, returns fonts (optionally filtered category). category Character. Filter category (e.g., "sans-serif", "serif", "display", "handwriting", "monospace"). Default: NULL (categories).

title: Search Bunny Fonts by Name or Category (Direct Function) — font_search_bunny
text: data.frame matching font metadata, empty data.frame matches.

title: Search Bunny Fonts by Name or Category (Direct Function) — font_search_bunny
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Search for fonts with "sans" in the name font_search_bunny("sans")  # Search within a specific category font_search_bunny("roboto", category = "sans-serif")  # List all monospace fonts font_search_bunny(category = "monospace")  # List all available fonts font_search_bunny() } # }

title: Get Font Cache Directory — get_font_cache_dir
text: Returns directory fonts /cached. Uses platform-appropriate cache locations via rappdirs package.

title: Get Font Cache Directory — get_font_cache_dir
code: get_font_cache_dir()

title: Get Font Cache Directory — get_font_cache_dir
text: (: character(1)) Absolute path fonts cache directory.

title: Get Font Cache Directory — get_font_cache_dir
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get font cache directory cache_dir <- get_font_cache_dir() print(cache_dir) } # }
